H-OS-L1.name: Pebble
H-OS-L1.sphere: H-OS-L1 (n=95, mean=7.15, std=1.78, confidence=strong) delivers top overstimulation relief. Qualitative data highlights grounding effects and professional use (therapists:5, caregivers:6). Strong unimodal distribution. YouTube views=30 (highest in library) but retention data unavailable. As overstimulation zone leader, AutoGluon predicts 6.859 — slightly below observed mean, indicating real-world performance exceeds model expectations.
H-OS-L1.sphere_plain: H-OS-L1 excels at reducing overload. Most users feel calmer within minutes, especially during caregiving.

H-OS-L2.name: Rainfall

H-OS-L3.name: Glacier
H-OS-L3.sphere: H-OS-L3 (n=47, mean=7.04, std=1.68, confidence=meaningful) shows anomalous overstimulation results. Qualitative data confirms grounding benefits but notes 10% zero scores. Distribution reveals unexpected low-score cluster despite DESCENT design. YouTube views low (8). Not zone leader; no AutoGluon prediction available.
H-OS-L3.sphere_plain: H-OS-L3 helps with overload but some users report no benefit — worth understanding why results vary.

sphere_commentary.overview:
The three series show expected variance patterns: HEALING is most consistent, THRIVING achieves highest mean scores in specific protocols, and TRANSFORMING exhibits greatest spread aligned with its design for low-affect states.
• HEALING demonstrates lowest variance (std=1.77) indicating consistent responses across protocols.
• THRIVING achieves highest mean scores in Focus & Clarity (e.g., T-FC-L3 mean=7.63).
• TRANSFORMING shows highest variance (std=1.87) and lowest mean (6.59), reflecting work with variable readiness states.
sphere_commentary.cross_series:
Series exhibit distinct patterns aligned with design intent: HEALING (DESCENT) excels in consistency, THRIVING (HOLD) in peak efficacy for focus, and TRANSFORMING (mixed ASCENT/DESCENT) in variable response across readiness states.
• HEALING protocols (mostly DESCENT) show lowest variance, indicating reliable nervous system regulation.
• THRIVING protocols (all HOLD) achieve highest mean scores, particularly in Focus & Clarity (T-FC-L3).
• TRANSFORMING protocols exhibit greatest spread, reflecting work with low-affect states where readiness varies.
sphere_commentary.anomalies:
Several protocols show irregular patterns requiring investigation: low mean/high variance in TRANSFORMING mind clarity, elevated variance in THRIVING maintenance, bimodal distribution in top focus protocol, and unexpected zero scores in overstimulation.
• X-MC-L3: mean=5.7, std=2.45 (n=10) — lowest mean and highest variability, suggesting population or Hz mismatch.
• T-MB-L2: mean=6.68, std=2.03 (n=22) — unusually high variance for THRIVING maintenance, indicating inconsistent response.
• T-FC-L3: mean=7.63, std=1.97 (n=35) — highest mean but bimodal distribution, suggesting user split.
• H-OS-L3: mean=7.04, 10% zero scores (n=47) — unexpected for DESCENT overstimulation protocol.
sphere_commentary.development_signals:
Development signals highlight refinements based on efficacy patterns, usage insights, and variability reduction.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] T-FC-L3: High efficacy and YouTube reach suggest investigating its success to inform other Focus protocol refinements.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] X-ME-L3: Frequent AutoGluon cross-bucket recommendations to H-Zones warrant testing its use in anxiety protocols.
• H-CR-L3: High response variability suggests testing increased hold phase to stabilize crisis intervention outcomes.
• T-MB-L2: Elevated standard deviation indicates reviewing adviser routing for better listener-protocol matching in Maintenance.
• H-SL-L2: Consider caregiver-specific adjustments, as primary use appears to be supported listening rather than self-use.
• X-MC-L3: Assess entire Mind & Clarity entry in TRANSFORMING for potential redesign or revised listener matching due to consistently low performance.
sphere_commentary.embedding_analysis:
Significant cross-run drift indicates evolving analytical understanding, particularly for THRIVING maintenance and focus protocols. Strong semantic clustering within X-MD (motivation) and H-AX (anxiety) pairs supports design coherence, while H-SL-L2, T-MB-L2, and H-OS-L1 remain analytically distinct, suggesting potential misalignment with series narratives.
• CROSS-RUN DRIFT: T-MB-L2 shows lowest similarity (0.612), indicating substantial analytical shift; 21+ protocols exceed drift threshold.
• CROSS-PROTOCOL SEMANTIC CLUSTERS: X-MD-L1/L2 (0.861) and H-AX-L1/L2 (0.860) are most similar pairs, reinforcing series design.
• Most analytically distinct: H-SL-L2, T-MB-L2, H-OS-L1 (mean similarity <0.68) suggest potential framing misalignment.

by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L2: H-CR-L2 is a dependable crisis tool. Users notice improvement despite starting from high distress.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L3: H-CR-L3 supports crisis recovery with mixed results. Some feel quick relief while others need more time.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L1: H-OS-L1 excels at reducing overload. Most users feel calmer within minutes, especially during caregiving.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L2: H-OS-L2 manages overload effectively. Users report feeling grounded and better able to cope.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L3: H-OS-L3 helps with overload but some users report no benefit — worth understanding why results vary.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L1: H-SL-L1 works well for sleep. Most users fall asleep faster and report waking up refreshed.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L2: H-SL-L2 supports sleep with mixed results. Some users find it very helpful while others feel less effect.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L3: H-SL-L3 aids relaxation for sleep. Users appreciate its calming rhythm and steady support.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L1: T-CF-L1 encourages creative flow with modest results. Users feel slightly to moderately more creative.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L2: T-CF-L2 supports steady creative flow. Users feel moderately more creative and focused.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L3: T-CF-L3 strongly boosts creative flow. Users feel significantly more creative, especially during focused work.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L1: T-FC-L1 helps with focus and clarity. Users notice improved concentration during tasks.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L2: T-FC-L2 enhances focus reliably. Users feel less distracted and more attentive.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L3: T-FC-L3 sharply improves focus. Users report significant clarity gains, though some use it while multitasking.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L1: T-MB-L1 supports emotional balance. Users feel steadier and more grounded in daily life.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L2: T-MB-L2 shows mixed balance results. Some users feel much steadier while others notice less change.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L3: T-MB-L3 maintains emotional steadiness. Users report feeling balanced and calm after use.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L1: X-MC-L1 aids clear thinking. Users report improved focus, especially during work or study.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L2: X-MC-L2 supports mental clarity. Users feel more focused and alert during focused tasks.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L3: X-MC-L3 has mixed clarity results. Some users feel clearer while others feel foggy or unfocused.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L1: X-MD-L1 boosts motivation and drive. Users report increased energy and goal-directed behavior.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L2: X-MD-L2 offers modest motivation support. Users feel slight increases in energy and initiative.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L3: X-MD-L3 varies in motivation effects. Some users feel energized while others feel calm or sleepy.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L1: X-ME-L1 reliably improves mood and energy. Users feel more positive and energetic after use.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L2: X-ME-L2 enhances mood consistently. Users report feeling more positive and energetic.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L3: X-ME-L3 shows mixed mood results. Some users feel uplifted while others notice little change.
